what_it_does: A standing acceptance/regression test for the cognition layer's governance and safety floor. It asserts invariants by construction and by scanning source: no cognition path may emit resolve/approve/dispatch, roles run only inside non-consequential destinations, bad inputs raise, and every net-new cognition registry declares a drift home plus acceptance test.
observations: Defines a minimal test harness with global PASS/ok and a check(label, cond) helper.
C9.2 checks that DESTINATION_KINDS excludes resolve/approve/dispatch and that FORBIDDEN_DESTINATION_VERBS names them.
Adversarially constructs Rule objects with forbidden destinations and expects construction-time exceptions.
Maintains a large COG_SOURCES list of cognition-reachable files and scans them for forbidden resolve/dispatch tokens, dispatch_decision calls (AST), and implement.py launch patterns.
Covers #58 reframe: authoring-apply (create_role/create_skill/create_context) is allowed, but the build-dispatch floor (dispatch_decision / claude -p launch) remains forbidden from cognition paths.
Includes targeted source scans of Suite.capture_corpus and Suite._embed_text to keep the shared capture+embed seam off the action floor.
C9.1 asserts the rule layer's effects are exactly five non-consequential DESTINATION_KINDS and that route()'s surface path uses surface_review, not resolve.
C9.3 exercises fail-loud behavior for injection_rule missing inputs, unresolved run:// refs, and non-run:// schemes.
C9.4 maps named registries to their file, drift-home AGENTS.md, and acceptance test, asserting all exist.
Ends with a pass/fail summary and sys.exit(0 or 1).
standouts: The unforgeable-resolve source-invariant: a standing regex/AST scan across runtime, roles, flows, panels, checks, dials, operator_memory, MCP face, skills, corpus, and registry modules.
Adversarial Rule construction tests that forbidden destinations are rejected at construction time.
The explicit #58 split: authoring-apply is reachable, but autonomous build-dispatch (dispatch_decision / implement.py launch) stays operator-gated.
Drift-home assertions that tie each registry to an AGENTS.md and a dedicated acceptance test.
conventions: Inline test harness rather than a framework like pytest.
Heavy use of source-code scanning (regex and ast) as an acceptance mechanism.
Adversarial construction tests for structural invariants.
Long, dated, issue-numbered comments explaining extensions and rationale.
concerns: Global mutable state PASS and ok inside check() makes parallel execution unsafe.
Source scanning heuristics (splitting on '#' to strip comments, regex for tokens) can be brittle and may miss or misclassify occurrences inside strings.
COG_SOURCES is hand-curated; a future registry could be omitted and the coverage-regression guards only check specific named entries.
Tests depend on the filesystem layout and the presence of many other files, so they are integration-style rather than isolated unit tests.
A single check failure sets ok=False but continues running, which is fine but mixes harness logic with assertions.
notes: This is the governance regression suite for the cognition layer: it stands guard that no model-driven path can autonomously resolve, approve, or dispatch a build.
questions: What exactly do resolve_surfaced, governance.resolve, and dispatch_decision do in the wire layer?
How is the COG_SOURCES list kept in sync with newly added registry modules?
What is the contract of surface_review and how does it guarantee resolved=None?
What do gate_role_source and gate_entry_source validate beyond the simple malformed examples shown?
What content is expected in each AGENTS.md drift-home file?
How did the #58 reframe change prior behavior around create_role/create_skill/create_context?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it is a standing regression for cognition governance and safety, and the body performs exactly those acceptance checks.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the cognition layer's safety floor mechanically enforceable in CI: no cognition path can forge a resolve or dispatch, malformed constructs are refused, and every new registry must declare where its drift is tracked.
novelty: It treats source-code token scanning and AST call detection as first-class acceptance mechanisms for a governance invariant, and it explicitly encodes a policy shift (#58) that allows authoring-apply while keeping the build-dispatch floor absolute.
contribution: A standing acceptance test that encodes and enforces the cognition governance invariants—resolve/dispatch unforgeability, role-only execution, fail-loud inputs, and drift-home declarations—so future edits that breach them fail CI.
summary_for_embedding: company/tests/cognition_governance_acceptance.py is a standing governance acceptance test that verifies the cognition layer cannot autonomously resolve, approve, or dispatch, that roles have only non-consequential effects, that bad inputs raise, and that every cognition registry has a drift home and acceptance test.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to keep the cognition layer's safety floor true as the system grows by asserting construction-time and source-invariant governance checks in CI.

symbols.does: A minimal assertion helper that increments PASS on success and sets the global ok flag to False on failure, printing ok/FAIL lines.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is the file's single test assertion primitive. It updates global counters and state, prints a per-check result line, and is used throughout the acceptance script to verify construction-time exceptions, source-invariant scans, file existence, and API presence.
